prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `1d398264`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `1d398264` in [__init__.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1d398264/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1d398264/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1d398264/generator.py#L20), and [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1d398264/notes.md). The rule is: keep the 3x3 nonzero block in place, treat its center as the origin, and extend each of the eight surrounding cells outward in its own compass direction as a straight ray. No new primitive was needed.

`opus46` was consistent with the official examples and I used it as the starting hypothesis. `sonnet45` was discarded and documented in [notes.md](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_1d398264/notes.md) because it incorrectly describes only leftward propagation. The generator matches the task family by sampling square zero-background grids, placing a single dense 3x3 colored block away from the borders, using a small repeated outer-color palette with a distinct center, and constructing outputs from those eight directional rays rather than from the verifier.

Verification passed end-to-end: `5/5` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1d398264/task.json), [originals.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1d398264/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1d398264/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1d398264/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/1d398264/previews/preview_03.png). There isn’t a terminal PNG renderer installed here, so the final sanity check was done by inspecting representative generated grids directly rather than opening the PNG sheet itself.
